name: Exploit: Exploits Followed by Firewall Accepts
notes: This rule triggers when exploit or attack events are followed by firewall accept events, which may indicate a successful attack.
bb_references: BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch in order from the same source IP from the previous sequence
conditions_text: when a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans, in order, with the same destination IP followed by a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch in order from the same source IP from the previous sequence, within 10 minutes
thresholds: when a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans, in order, with the same destination IP followed by a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch in order from the same source IP from the previous sequence, within 10 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Exploit: Exploits Followed by Firewall Accepts on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans, in order, with the same destination IP followed by a subset of at least 1 of these BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept, BB:DeviceDefinition: FW / Router / Switch in order from the same source IP from the previous sequence, within 10 minutes
RED_list: Event description: Detects when exploit or attack events are followed by firewall accept events, which may indicate a successful attack.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
